groups.context_description: Term names the progressive inner renewal of the self — the ongoing transformation of the inner person toward God's image, expressed as daily renewal of the inner self and growth in knowledge

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Cor 4:16 So we do not lose heart . Though our outer self is wasting away , our inner self is being renewed day by day .

groups.context_description: Term names the renewal of the inner person — the mind transformed by the Spirit as the foundation for discerning God's will and living transformed

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 12:2 Do not be conformed to this world , but be transformed by the renewal of your mind , that by testing you may discern what is the will of God , what is good and acceptable and perfect .

groups.context_description: Term names the perishing as those whose vulnerable condition calls forth advocacy from the inner conscience of others — the destitute whose cause requires the advocate's will

anchor_verses.verse_text: Pro 31:8 Open your mouth for the mute , for the rights of all who are destitute .

groups.context_description: Term names change as inner transformation — the hoped-for renewal of one's condition through patient waiting, or the failure to change as evidence of persistent inner hardness toward God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 14:14 If a man dies , shall he live again? All the days of my service I would wait , till my renewal should come .

groups.context_description: Term names the Nazirite locks of hair as the physical expression of an inner covenantal reality — the consecration whose physical removal represents the departure of inner strength and divine empowerment

anchor_verses.verse_text: Judg 16:13 Then Delilah said to Samson , “ Until now you have mocked me and told me lies . Tell me how you might be bound .” And he said to her, “ If you weave the seven locks of my head with the web and fasten it tight with the pin ^ , then I shall become weak and be like any other man .”

groups.context_description: Term describes the inner transformation of the person toward conformity with God — renewal of mind and progressive conformity to divine glory

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 12:2 Do not be conformed to this world , but be transformed by the renewal of your mind , that by testing you may discern what is the will of God , what is good and acceptable and perfect .

groups.context_description: Term describes Christ's visible transformation, which mediates an inner encounter between the disciples and divine reality

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mar 9:2 And after six days Jesus took with him Peter and James and John , and led them up a high mountain by themselves . And he was transfigured before them ,

groups.context_description: Term describes the interior formation of Christ within the person — a process of inner shaping toward Christ-likeness

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gal 4:19 my little children , for whom I am again in the anguish of childbirth until Christ is formed in you !

groups.context_description: Term describes the inner renewal of strength and vitality that comes through waiting on or turning to God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 40:31 but they who wait for the Lord shall renew their strength ; they shall mount up with wings like eagles ; they shall run and not be weary ; they shall walk and not faint .

groups.context_description: Term expresses the transience of human life and creation — the rapid passing that characterises human existence

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 90:5 You sweep them away as with a flood ; they are like a dream , like grass that is renewed in the morning :

groups.context_description: Term describes the wilful overriding of divine covenant — an inner act of defiance against binding obligation

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 24:5 The earth lies defiled under its inhabitants ; for they have transgressed the laws , violated the statutes , broken the everlasting covenant .

groups.context_description: Term describes the passing of God or divine spirit near the person, engaging the person's inner capacity for perception or producing inner dread

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 9:11 Behold, he passes by me, and I see him not; he moves on , but I do not perceive him .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner darkness that fills the person when their inner orientation is corrupted — the whole inner life darkened by a bad eye

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mat 6:23 but if your eye is bad , your whole body will be full of darkness . If then the light in you is darkness , how great is the darkness !

groups.context_description: Term names darkness as the inner condition of spiritual alienation — the state of the person who hates, does not know where they are going, and has not come to the light

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Jo 2:11 But whoever hates his brother is in the darkness and walks in the darkness , and does not know where he is going , because the darkness has blinded his eyes .

groups.context_description: Term names the darkness of concealment — the inner life hidden in secret, which will be brought to light

anchor_verses.verse_text: Luk 12:3 Therefore whatever you have said in the dark shall be heard in the light , and what you have whispered in private rooms shall be proclaimed on the housetops .

groups.context_description: Term describes the darkening of inner capacities — understanding, heart, and eyes — as the consequence of wilful rejection of God or as divine judicial act

anchor_verses.verse_text: Eph 4:18 They are darkened in their understanding , alienated from the life of God because of the ignorance that is in them , due to their hardness of heart .

groups.context_description: Term names darkness as the inner-spiritual domain the person inhabits, is rescued from, or identifies with — their fundamental spiritual condition and identity

anchor_verses.verse_text: Eph 5:8 for at one time you were darkness , but now you are light in the Lord . Walk as children of light

groups.context_description: Term names darkness as the moral domain of works and powers the person either participates in or is called to renounce

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 13:12 The night is far gone ; the day is at hand . So then let us cast off the works of darkness and put on the armor of light .

groups.context_description: Term names the darkness of the inner life — hidden purposes, corrupted inner light, self-deception about one's inner state

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Cor 4:5 Therefore do not pronounce judgment before the time , before the Lord comes , who will bring to light the things now hidden in darkness and will disclose the purposes of the heart . Then each one will receive his commendation from God .

groups.context_description: Term names the outer darkness of eschatological judgment — the ultimate inner-being consequence of exclusion from God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mat 22:13 Then the king said to the attendants , ‘ Bind him hand and foot and cast him into the outer darkness . In that place there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth .’

groups.context_description: Term describes the plunging of the person/domain into darkness as divine judgment, producing inner anguish

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rev 16:10 The fifth angel poured out his bowl on the throne of the beast , and its kingdom was plunged into darkness . People gnawed their tongues in anguish

groups.context_description: Term names spiritual blindness as the inner condition of the person who claims to see but does not — self-deception, hardness, and inability to perceive spiritual reality

anchor_verses.verse_text: Joh 9:41 Jesus said to them , “ If you were blind , you would have no guilt ; but now that you say , ‘We see ,’ your guilt remains .

groups.context_description: Term names physical blindness as the condition of need healed by the Messiah — a sign that calls forth inner-being faith and witnesses to spiritual reality

anchor_verses.verse_text: Luk 4:18 “The Spirit of the Lord is upon me , because he has anointed me to proclaim good news to the poor . He has sent me to proclaim liberty to the captives and recovering of sight to the blind , to set at liberty those who are oppressed ,

groups.context_description: Term describes the act of blinding the inner capacities — eyes and mind — whether through hatred, spiritual adversary, or divine judicial action

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Cor 4:4 In their case the god of this world has blinded the minds of the unbelievers , to keep them from seeing the light of the gospel of the glory of Christ , who is the image of God .

groups.context_description: Spiritual deadness as the person's condition — dead in sin, dead to God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mat 23:27 “ Woe to you , scribes and Pharisees , hypocrites ! For you are like whitewashed tombs , which outwardly appear beautiful , but within are full of dead people’s bones and all uncleanness .
Eph 2:1 And you were dead in the trespasses and sins

groups.context_description: Dead to sin / dead to the law — inner-being reorientation through Christ

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 6:11 So you also must consider yourselves dead to sin and alive to God in Christ Jesus .

Rom 7:4 Likewise , my brothers , you also have died to the law through the body of Christ , so that you may belong to another , to him who has been raised from the dead , in order that we may bear fruit for God .

groups.context_description: Dead works — works from spiritual deadness; conscience purified from them

anchor_verses.verse_text: Heb 6:1 Therefore let us leave the elementary doctrine of Christ and go on to maturity , not laying again a foundation of repentance from dead works and of faith toward God ,
Heb 9:14 how much more will the blood of Christ , who through the eternal Spirit offered himself without blemish to God , purify our conscience from dead works to serve the living God .

groups.context_description: Dead faith — faith as a dead inner-being capacity without works

anchor_verses.verse_text: Jam 2:17 So also faith by itself , if it does not have works , is dead .

groups.context_description: Resurrection of the dead as ground for inner-being hope, faith, and aspiration

anchor_verses.verse_text: Phili 3:11 that by any means possible I may attain the resurrection from the dead .

1Pe 1:3 Blessed be the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ ! According to his great mercy , he has caused us to be born again to a living hope through the resurrection of Jesus Christ from the dead ,

groups.context_description: Deliberate mortification of inner-being capacities — putting to death what is earthly

anchor_verses.verse_text: Col 3:5 Put to death therefore what is earthly in you: sexual immorality , impurity , passion , evil desire , and covetousness , which is idolatry .

groups.context_description: Death as consequence of inner moral failure — dying as outcome of folly, wickedness, sin

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gen 2:17 but of the tree of the knowledge of good and evil you shall not eat , for in the day that you eat of it you shall surely die .”
Job 5:2 Surely vexation kills the fool , and jealousy slays the simple .

groups.context_description: Death as object of inner-being orientation — fear, longing, resolve, wisdom on mortality

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 21:25 Another dies in bitterness of soul , never having tasted of prosperity .

Jon 4:8 When the sun rose , God appointed a scorching east wind , and the sun beat down on the head of Jonah so that he was faint . And he asked that he might die and said , “It is better for me to die than to live .”

groups.context_description: God's sovereignty over death as ground of inner-being trust and confidence

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Sa 2:6 The Lord kills and brings to life ; he brings down to Sheol and raises up .
Hab 1:12 Are you not from everlasting , O Lord my God , my Holy One? We shall not die . O Lord , you have ordained them as a judgment , and you, O Rock , have established them for reproof .

groups.context_description: The dead in relation to God — cut off from his hand; loss of praise capacity

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 88:5 like one set loose among the dead , like the slain that lie in the grave , like those whom you remember no more , for they are cut off from your hand .
Psa 88:10 Do you work wonders for the dead ? Do the departed rise up to praise you? Selah

groups.context_description: Death as end/consequence of a moral path — wickedness leads to death; choosing life vs death

anchor_verses.verse_text: Deu 30:19 I call heaven and earth to witness against you today , that I have set before you life and death , blessing and curse . Therefore choose life , that you and your offspring may live ,
Pro 8:36 but he who fails to find me injures himself ; all who hate me love death .”

groups.context_description: Death as power/threat/reality the person faces, fears, chooses, or is delivered from

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Sa 20:3 But David vowed again , saying , “Your father knows well that I have found favor in your eyes , and he thinks , ‘Do not let Jonathan know this , lest he be grieved .’ But truly , as the Lord lives and as your soul lives , there is but a step between me and death .”
Psa 55:4 My heart is in anguish within me; the terrors of death have fallen upon me .

groups.context_description: God's sovereignty over death as ground of redemption and inner-being hope

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 25:8 He will swallow up death forever ; and the Lord God will wipe away tears from all faces , and the reproach of his people he will take away from all the earth , for the Lord has spoken .
Hos 13:14 I shall ransom them from the power of Sheol ; I shall redeem them from Death . O Death , where are your plagues ? O Sheol , where is your sting ? Compassion is hidden from my eyes .

groups.context_description: The dead and inner-being capacities — those who no longer praise, remember, or are remembered

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 6:5 For in death there is no remembrance of you; in Sheol who will give you praise ?
Psa 116:15 Precious in the sight of the Lord is the death of his saints .

groups.context_description: Death as spiritual/moral condition — separation from God, consequence of sin

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 8:6 For to set the mind on the flesh is death , but to set the mind on the Spirit is life and peace .
1Jo 3:14 We know that we have passed out of death into life , because we love the brothers . Whoever does not love abides in death .

groups.context_description: Death as power/threat that dominates or enslaves through fear

anchor_verses.verse_text: Heb 2:15 and deliver all those who through fear of death were subject to lifelong slavery .

groups.context_description: Believer's inner identification with Christ's death as transformative reality

anchor_verses.verse_text: Phili 2:8 And being found in human form , he humbled himself by becoming obedient to the point of death , even death on a cross .
Phili 3:10 that I may know him and the power of his resurrection , and may share his sufferings , becoming like him in his death ,

groups.context_description: Faithful inner-being orientation toward death — perseverance, defiance, hope

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Cor 15:55 “O death , where is your victory ? O death , where is your sting ?”

Rev 2:10 Do not fear what you are about to suffer . Behold , the devil is about to throw some of you into prison , that you may be tested , and for ten days you will have tribulation . Be faithful unto death , and I will give you the crown of life .

groups.context_description: Death as measure of extreme inner suffering — soul sorrowful even to death

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mar 14:34 And he said to them , “ My soul is very sorrowful , even to death . Remain here and watch .”

groups.context_description: Spiritual deadness in a living person — physically alive but inwardly dead through self-indulgence

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Ti 5:6 but she who is self-indulgent is dead even while she lives .

groups.context_description: Mortal body as site of inner-being orientation, longing, and moral struggle

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 6:12 Let not sin therefore reign in your mortal body , to make you obey its passions .
2Cor 5:4 For while we are still in this tent , we groan , being burdened — not that we would be unclothed , but that we would be further clothed , so that what is mortal may be swallowed up by life .

groups.context_description: Inner-being commitment of dying together — loyalty and union through shared death including Christ

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Cor 7:3 I do not say this to condemn you, for I said before that you are in our hearts , to die together and to live together .
2Ti 2:11 The saying is trustworthy , for : If we have died with him, we will also live with him;
